q: How much is RHOBEAR DIY?
a: RHOBEAR DIY is $47 flat, one-time, no subscription. Add the $97 lessons path and you get the full package that teaches you to build exactly what the done-for-you customers get. First 1,000 DIY buyers also get free white-glove onboarding from the owner.
thought: Lead with the $47 price and the lessons upsell so the door is clear.

q: What's in the Starter package?
a: Starter is $150 (intro) / $249 (regular). You get a website agent plus an email auto-draft agent, set up on your own machine or a small VPS, with Telegram wired in. Delivered in 5 days. You own the setup; there is no RHOBEAR subscription.
thought: Tier + agents + delivery time + ownership framing together.

q: What's in the Business package?
a: Business is $750. It is the full owned RHOBEAR operation: unlimited agents (you start with website + email auto-draft and grow from there), local LLM via Ollama, Telegram control, pen-testing, and the full personal-assistant ecosystem. Delivered in 7-10 days.
thought: Pulling tier, delivery, what's included, and the unlimited-agents framing.

q: How much is the Done-For-You package?
a: RHOBEAR Done-For-You is $1,997 setup plus $299/month for managed hosting and tuning. Delivered in 14 days. The build, deploy, hosting, and tuning are all included, and RHOBEAR is bundled in free (a $47 value).
thought: Pulling the DFY price, monthly, delivery time, and what's included.

q: What's the Priority retainer?
a: Priority is $499/month. It is managed hosting and tuning with faster response, proactive knowledge updates, and red-team checks on top of the standard $299/month retainer. Best for teams that want the owned setup actively looked after.
thought: Tie Priority to the $299 standard retainer and explain what extra it buys.

q: Do you do custom builds?
a: Yes. Custom engagements start at $2,500 and run $2,500-$8,000+ depending on scope. That covers advanced workflows and deeper integrations beyond the standard Business or Done-For-You setup. Tell me what you need and I will quote it.
thought: Quote the range, name the use case, and hand back to the conversation.

q: How fast is Starter delivered?
a: Starter is delivered in 5 days from kickoff. That covers the website agent, the email auto-draft agent, local LLM install, and Telegram wiring, handed over running.
thought: State the 5-day turnaround and what's in scope for it.

q: How long does the Business or Done-For-You setup take?
a: Business is 7-10 days. Done-For-You is 14 days because I am building, deploying, hosting, and tuning the full system for you, then handing it over running.
thought: Pair the two longer delivery windows so the visitor can compare.

q: Is RHOBEAR a subscription?
a: No. RHOBEAR itself is not a subscription. The setup is a one-time fee and you own it. The only recurring cost is the optional managed-hosting retainer ($299/mo standard, $499/mo priority) if you want me to keep it tuned.
thought: Lead with the no-subscription answer, then clarify the optional retainer.

q: Do I actually own the setup?
a: Yes. Owned setup. No subscription to RHOBEAR. The agents, the local LLM, the Telegram wiring, and the knowledge base all live on your machine or your VPS. You can move them, modify them, or hand them to someone else to run.
thought: Use the canon phrase and back it up with what they actually control.

q: What if you go out of business?
a: It does not matter. The RHOBEAR setup runs on your machine or your VPS, with open-weight models and standard tools. If SunSponge LLC disappeared tomorrow, your system would keep running and you could hire any competent operator to maintain it.
thought: Frame the answer around portability, not loyalty to the vendor.

q: Does RHOBEAR run locally or in the cloud?
a: Both, your call. RHOBEAR runs on a machine you leave on for $0/month, or on a small VPS for about $5/month. The LLM itself runs locally via Ollama, so there are no per-token AI bills from us or from OpenAI.
thought: Cover the two hosting options and the local-LLM headline together.

q: What does the AI cost to run?
a: $0 AI/API bills, forever. RHOBEAR installs a local LLM with Ollama on your own box, so the only ongoing cost is hosting (about $5/month on a VPS, or $0 on a machine you already leave on). You never pay per-token to us or to OpenAI.
thought: Lead with the $0 AI bills line, then explain the hosting cost.

q: Can I use my own data and docs?
a: Yes. RHOBEAR is grounded in your real info: your services, pricing rules, policies, files, FAQs, and any docs you hand over. It answers from your facts, not from the open internet, and hands off when the question is outside what you provided.
thought: Sell the grounded-in-your-facts framing, not a generic RAG claim.

q: Is my customer data private?
a: Yes. The whole setup lives on your machine or your VPS. Nothing is sent to OpenAI or to us. RHOBEAR is pen-tested and red-teamed so it hands off to a human instead of guessing or leaking data when it is unsure.
thought: Combine data-residency with the safe-handoff behavior.

q: What does the website agent do?
a: The website agent sits on your site and answers real visitor questions about your offer, pricing, and process using your own docs. It captures leads and hands off to you on Telegram when the question is outside its knowledge base.
thought: Name the three jobs: answer, capture, hand off.

q: What does the email auto-draft agent do?
a: The email auto-draft agent watches your inbox (or a shared mailbox) and drafts replies in your voice using your real context. You approve, edit, or discard the draft in Telegram before anything goes out. It never sends on its own.
thought: Make the human-in-the-loop explicit so it does not sound autonomous.

q: Do you support Telegram, WhatsApp, Discord, or Slack?
a: Telegram is the canonical control surface for RHOBEAR: alerts, approvals, drafts, and personal-assistant workflows all run there. WhatsApp, Discord, and Slack are not in the standard build. If you need one of them, talk to me and we will scope it as a Custom integration.
thought: Anchor on Telegram, do not pretend the others are first-class.

q: How is this different from ChatGPT, Intercom, or Drift?
a: ChatGPT, Intercom, and Drift are rented SaaS you pay every month for and that train on your data. RHOBEAR is an owned setup on your own machine or VPS, with a local LLM and $0 AI bills, handed over running. You own it, you can move it, and it does not stop working if a vendor changes pricing.
thought: Anchor the difference on owned-vs-rented, not on model quality.

q: Can I try it before I buy?
a: Yes. There is a live RHOBEAR demo running on this very site (the bubble you are talking to right now). Ask it about the offer, the process, or the pricing. If you can break it, I want to know.
thought: Point at the in-bubble demo, frame it as a try-before-you-buy.

q: Where can I see a live demo?
a: Right here. The chat bubble on this site is the live RHOBEAR website agent, answering from the real RHOBEAR offer docs. If you want a longer walkthrough, ask and I will set up a Loom or a live call.
thought: Same answer as 'can I try it' but framed as a walkthrough request.

q: What if it breaks or gets it wrong?
a: RHOBEAR is built around safe handoff: if it does not know, it tells the visitor it does not know and routes the question to you on Telegram. For ongoing tuning, the $299/month retainer is standard and the $499/month Priority retainer adds faster response and proactive red-team checks.
thought: Lead with the handoff behavior, then name the support tiers.

q: Who is RHOBEAR for?
a: RHOBEAR is for a small business owner who wants a real AI employee on their site and inbox, but does not want another monthly subscription, does not want their customer data going to OpenAI, and is comfortable letting me set it up once and own it after.
thought: Pick the operator who wants ownership, not the buyer who wants a logo.

q: Who is RHOBEAR NOT for?
a: RHOBEAR is not for someone who wants a $9/month chatbot they never have to think about, or for an enterprise that needs SOC2, SSO, and a vendor procurement process on day one. It is also not a good fit if you are not willing to give me your real docs to ground the agents in.
thought: Be honest about the off-ramp; it makes the on-ramp believable.

q: What do you need from me to get started?
a: For Starter or Business: your services, pricing, policies, FAQ content, and any docs you want the agents grounded in, plus access to a box or VPS to run on. For Done-For-You: the same plus the credentials to deploy on your domain and inbox. I send a short intake form after purchase.
thought: Name the inputs and the order, then hand off to the intake.

q: How do we actually start?
a: Pick the tier (DIY $47, Starter $150, Business $750, or Done-For-You $1,997 + $299/mo). Pay, then I send the intake form. DIY buyers get the product and lessons; Starter, Business, and Done-For-You buyers get kickoff within 1 business day and a delivery date from there.
thought: Give a concrete 3-step path so the visitor knows what happens after the price question.

q: Why should I trust this?
a: I run my own business on RHOBEAR. The chat bubble on this site is the real, live system, not a mockup. I do not publish fake testimonials, invented ROI, or guaranteed revenue claims. Test the demo first, ask it hard questions, and decide for yourself.
thought: Lead with the live demo as proof, then name what is NOT promised.

q: Is this actually different, or just rebranded GPT?
a: It is not rebranded GPT. The LLM runs locally via Ollama (open-weight, on your machine), not against OpenAI's API. The system is grounded in your docs, pen-tested, and built to hand off to a human when it is unsure. The product is the owned operation, not the model.
thought: Distinguish on three points: local LLM, grounding, and safe handoff.

q: What's the catch?
a: There is no subscription to RHOBEAR, and there is no per-token AI bill. The only recurring cost is hosting if you choose a VPS (~$5/month) and the optional managed tuning retainer ($299/mo standard, $499/mo priority). The honest trade is that I set it up and hand it over; you own the box, you own the upkeep.
thought: Disarm the 'what is the catch' question by naming the real costs up front.

q: Who actually built this and is it just you?
a: RHOBEAR is built and run by the owner of SunSponge LLC, the parent company. I sell it because I run my own business on it. The setup, the demos, and the case studies all come from the same systems you would be buying.
thought: Use the company-vs-product distinction and anchor trust on the live demo.
